neighborhoods.name: Centro Historico
neighborhoods.description: Grand colonial center with cathedral, Paseo Montejo mansions, and a vibrant Mayan-Spanish cultural fusion
neighborhoods.character: Grand colonial, Paseo Montejo, cultural fusion
neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Mérida. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Garcia Gineres / Itzimna
neighborhoods.description: Established residential neighborhoods with tree-lined streets, parks, and medical facilities

housing.predominantType: Houses and apartments in established neighborhoods

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Mérida's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Merida Sur / Caucel
neighborhoods.description: Southern and western growth areas with new affordable housing developments

housing.predominantType: New construction houses and apartment developments

neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Santiago / Santa Ana
neighborhoods.description: Hip revitalized barrios north of the center with boutique hotels, restaurants, and expat-owned businesses
neighborhoods.character: Revitalized hip, boutique culture, expat businesses

housing.predominantType: Restored colonial houses and boutique apartments

neighborhoods.character_notes: Area with established international community and expat-oriented services. English is more commonly spoken here than in other neighborhoods.
